summary: This cluster investigates the interactive effects of solar ultraviolet radiation (UVR) and nutrient limitation on the photosynthetic performance of the marine diatom *Skeletonema costatum*. Researchers found that exposure to UV-A and UV-B significantly diminished photochemical quantum yield while increasing non-photochemical quenching, with these detrimental effects being exacerbated under nitrate-limited conditions. The studies collectively demonstrate that nutrient stress amplifies the photoinhibitory impact of UVR on this species, highlighting a critical ecological interaction in marine environments. The geographic scope is broadly relevant to coastal and oceanic waters where both UV penetration and nitrogen availability fluctuate.
